items.title: What this World Cup semifinal means to England and Argentina
items.summary: It is one of the biggest rivalries in men's World Cup history, and while the teams may downplay the history, England and Argentina fans are ready for another iconic showdown.

items.title: Labor aims to shape AI revolution with new data centre and AI copyright rules
items.summary: The prime minister unveils Labor's plan for new AI national standards, warning Australia has a narrow window to set AI's "social licence".

items.title: Tribunal told children had to use toilet in front of others in Cairns police watch house
items.summary: Advocates for young people say conditions in a Queensland watch house have not improved since three teenagers' human rights were breached. A tribunal heard the boys had limited privacy and had to use the toilet in front of others.

items.title: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates, after AEC announces redistribution
items.summary: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates under a long-awaited boundary redistribution announced by the Australian Electoral Commission.

items.title: Basil Zempilas declares any Labor loss in Secret Harbour by-election 'a win for Liberals'
items.summary: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, while indicating any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be "a victory for the Liberals".

items.title: Disgraced ex-footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame
items.summary: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

items.title: The sledge between Diamonds teammates that helped break the ice after Super Netball Grand Final
items.summary: Kate Moloney has had to put her team's Super Netball grand final nightmare aside to focus on the Commonwealth Games. Some gentle ribbing from her Aussie teammate has helped her see the lighter side.

items.title: Adelaide Parklands works to continue despite parliamentary inquiry
items.summary: Acting Premier Kyam Maher says works for the golf course redevelopment and MotoGP will continue despite a petition attracting 13,500 signatures.

items.title: The AFL is set on boutique stadiums for AFLW. Players think it is limiting the game
items.summary: The AFL has introduced four double-headers this season, meaning games will be played at bigger venues, while the league remains steadfast on boutique grounds for standalone matches.

items.title: Boy, 5, dies after being hit by 4WD in Cloverdale, in Perth's south-east
items.summary: A five-year-old boy dies and a 48-year-old woman is critically injured after being struck by a four-wheel drive outside a park in Cloverdale in Perth's south-east,

items.title: Timor-Leste scam centre compounds uncovered as police arrest Chinese, Indonesians, Cambodians
items.summary: Police in Timor-Leste uncover a major international scam centre in the country, arresting more than 300 Chinese, Indonesian and Cambodian nationals in raids across the capital, Dili.

items.title: Townsville gardener witnesses 'garbage guts' tree frog preying on sunbird
items.summary: A Townsville gardener was watering her garden when she witnessed a white-lipped tree frog catch a sunbird mid-flight.

items.summary: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

items.summary: Community members search for the injured animal, which was spooked off before wildlife volunteers could help.

items.title: US uses explosive one-way drone boats for first time in combat against Iran
items.summary: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

items.title: Flights bound for Adelaide cancelled and diverted due to thick fog
items.summary: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while others were either cancelled or diverted elsewhere.

items.title: Scottish traffic cone gets formal Boston welcome after Scots fans' World Cup adventures
items.summary: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

items.title: Adelaide Crows player Josh Worrell to face court over Port Road crash
items.summary: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

items.title: New political parties are exploiting Victoria's archaic electoral system
items.summary: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

items.title: World Cup: France falters at first real hurdle against Spain as superstars fail to shine
items.summary: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Lamine Yamal's streak and Folarin Balogun's confession

items.title: Aerial cull prompts push from sporting shooters to target feral deer in WA national parks
items.summary: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.

items.title: ASX rises after US reimposes naval blockade on Iran — as it happened

items.title: NRLW players hope NRL broadcast deal brings longer season, full-time salaries
items.summary: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

items.title: Australia grapples with child psychiatrist shortage as experts warn of consequences
items.summary: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

items.summary: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

items.title: AI-powered glasses improve everyday life for disability sector amid privacy concerns
items.summary: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

items.title: Two people killed in truck crash at Heathcote, north of Melbourne
items.summary: A car and truck collided about 9.45pm on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote.

items.title: 'People want community': Hobby-based clubs tackling loneliness
items.summary: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.

items.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon outlines plans for pine, cattle and tourism
items.summary: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

items.title: Fertiliser company Neutrog fined over smelly manure piles at Kanmantoo
items.summary: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

items.title: Australia's best-paid executive lives in US and earns 500 times that of average full-time worker
items.summary: Australia's most highly paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

items.title: Telstra compensation process puts onus on customers, consumer body says
items.summary: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

items.title: Japan ramps up defences amid concerns about China's 'military activities'
items.summary: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the middle of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

items.title: Batteries 'cheaper' than gas plants as data centres fuel turbine costs
items.summary: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

items.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels
items.summary: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

items.title: Impressionism's legacy reconsidered in exhibitions at AGSA and Geelong Gallery
items.summary: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

items.title: Art class uses creativity to paint different picture of mental health
items.summary: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

items.summary: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

items.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France
items.summary: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

items.title: Donald Trump ditches plan for fee in Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US trade new strikes
items.summary: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

items.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework
items.summary: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

items.title: Angus Taylor rules out One Nation coalition once and for all
items.summary: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with it.

items.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
items.summary: Tasmania's top health official says it will take the Launceston General Hospital "two or three days" to bring its services back to an acceptable level, after it issued a notice urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

items.title: PBS access to two vital MS treatments under threat
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations
items.summary: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Giant petrel found on Noosa Main Beach tests negative for H5 bird flu
items.summary: The giant petrel was taken to RSPCA Queensland's Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre where it underwent testing by Biosecurity Queensland as part of its surveillance program.

items.title: Family of motorcycle crash victim calls for careless driver to face prison time
items.summary: The family of a beloved member of the Northern Territory's motorsport community has urged a court to send a woman who failed to give way to him to jail over the fatal crash.

items.title: Woman strangled by wheelchair lap belt due to staff shortage, court hears
items.summary: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

items.title: Racism remains a key issue for AFL players while Indigenous AFLW players rate workplace culture low
items.summary: Racism remains a key issue for First Nations AFL players, while Indigenous AFLW players rate their workplace culture significantly lower than their peers, a new report finds.

items.title: Senior minister demands answers from Victoria's infrastructure authority following corruption allegations
items.summary: Victorian senior minister Melissa Horne says new allegations about union influence on state construction sites are "alarming", writing to the infrastructure authority to seek assurances they had acted appropriately.

items.title: Cyber warning over Russian hackers targeting critical industries
items.summary: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

items.title: Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating by Australian Road Assessment Program
items.summary: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

items.title: Mystery surrounds sudden departure of Mildura Base Public Hospital chief executive
items.summary: Mystery surrounds the departure of the chief executive of Mildura Base Public Hospital, who led the north-west Victorian hospital through its transition from private to public.

items.title: Jarome Luai joins Parramatta, Benji Marshall slams relationship breakdown rumours
items.summary: Benji Marshall has slammed "bull**** rumours" claiming a fractured relationship with Jarome Luai contributed to the star five-eighth's early exit from Wests Tigers.

items.title: Judge rules Donald Trump's settlement with IRS tried to 'manipulate the judicial process'
items.summary: US President Donald Trump's lawsuit against his own government over leaked tax records was an unparalleled exercise in self-dealing, a US judge says in a scathing ruling.
